primaryStudies.citation: Neelakantan H, Vance V, Wetzel MD, et al. Selective and membrane-permeable small molecule inhibitors of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase reverse high fat diet-induced obesity in mice. Biochem Pharmacol. 2018;147:141-152.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 5-Amino-1MQ reversed high-fat diet-induced obesity; reduced body weight by 7% in 11 days without affecting food intake; increased NAD+ levels in white adipose tissue; first demonstration of NNMT inhibitors for obesity treatment

primaryStudies.citation: Neelakantan H, Wang HY, Vance V, et al. Structure-Activity Relationship for Small Molecule Inhibitors of Nicotinamide N-Methyltransferase. J Med Chem. 2017;60(12):5015-5028.
primaryStudies.population: In vitro enzyme assays, cell culture
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Developed 5-amino-1MQ as potent, selective, membrane-permeable NNMT inhibitor; IC50 ~1.2 microM; excellent selectivity over other methyltransferases; reduced lipid accumulation in 3T3-L1 adipocytes

primaryStudies.citation: Kraus D, Yang Q, Kong D, et al. Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase knockdown protects against diet-induced obesity. Nature. 2014;508(7495):258-262.
primaryStudies.population: NNMT knockout mice, human adipose tissue samples
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Landmark study establishing NNMT as obesity target; NNMT knockdown in white adipose tissue protects against diet-induced obesity; NNMT regulates adipose tissue NAD+ levels and energy expenditure; NNMT elevated in obese human adipose tissue

primaryStudies.citation: Brachs S, Polber S, Gluber S, et al. Genetic Nicotinamide N-Methyltransferase (Nnmt) Deficiency in Male Mice Improves Insulin Sensitivity in Diet-Induced Obesity. Diabetes. 2019;68(3):527-538.
primaryStudies.population: NNMT-deficient male mice on high-fat diet
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT deficiency improved insulin sensitivity; reduced body weight and fat mass on high-fat diet; enhanced energy expenditure; supports NNMT as therapeutic target

primaryStudies.citation: Hong S, Moreno-Navarrete JM, Wei X, et al. Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase regulates hepatic nutrient metabolism through Sirt1 protein stabilization. Nat Med. 2015;21(8):887-894.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT knockdown in liver increases NAD+ and activates Sirt1; protects against hepatic steatosis; improves glucose tolerance; links NNMT to NAD+-sirtuin metabolic regulation

primaryStudies.citation: Kannt A, Pfenninger A, Teber L, et al. Association of nicotinamide-N-methyltransferase mRNA expression in human adipose tissue and the plasma concentration of its product, 1-methylnicotinamide, with insulin resistance. Diabetologia. 2015;58(4):799-808.
primaryStudies.population: Human subjects with varying insulin sensitivity
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT expression in human adipose tissue correlates with insulin resistance; plasma 1-methylnicotinamide elevated in insulin resistant subjects; supports NNMT as human metabolic target

primaryStudies.citation: Ulanovskaya OA, Zuhl AM, Cravatt BF. NNMT promotes epigenetic remodeling in cancer by creating a metabolic methylation sink. Nat Chem Biol. 2013;9(5):300-306.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT creates a metabolic methylation sink; consumes SAM and produces SAH; affects histone methylation; potential implications for cancer and metabolism

primaryStudies.citation: Ruf S, Hallur MS, Engber TM, et al. Novel nicotinamide analog as potent inhibitor of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase. Bioorg Med Chem Lett. 2018;28(5):922-925.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Development of alternative NNMT inhibitor scaffolds; confirms NNMT as druggable target; supports ongoing medicinal chemistry efforts

primaryStudies.citation: Pissios P. Nicotinamide N-Methyltransferase: More Than a Vitamin B3 Clearance Enzyme. Trends Endocrinol Metab. 2017;28(5):340-353.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of NNMT biology; discusses role in energy metabolism, adiposity, and NAD+ homeostasis; identifies NNMT inhibition as promising obesity intervention

primaryStudies.citation: Roberti A, Fernandez AF, Fraga MF. Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase: At the crossroads between cellular metabolism and epigenetic regulation. Mol Metab. 2021;45:101165.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Updated review of NNMT at intersection of metabolism and epigenetics; discusses NAD+/SAM balance; summarizes therapeutic potential of NNMT inhibitors including 5-amino-1MQ

primaryStudies.citation: Campagna R, Mateuszuk L, Wojnar-Lason K, et al. Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase in endothelium protects against oxidant stress-induced endothelial injury. Biochim Biophys Acta Mol Cell Res. 2021;1868(10):119082.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT may have protective role in vascular endothelium; cautions that systemic NNMT inhibition may have cardiovascular considerations; important safety signal

primaryStudies.citation: Schmeisser K, Mansfeld J, Kuhlow D, et al. Role of sirtuins in lifespan regulation is linked to methylation of nicotinamide. Nat Chem Biol. 2013;9(11):693-700.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: NNMT affects lifespan through methylation of nicotinamide; connects NNMT activity to sirtuin regulation and longevity pathways

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not approved; no regulatory submissions; research chemical only
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: All efficacy data from animal studies; no human clinical trials published; 5-amino-1MQ is a small molecule, not a peptide; long-term safety unknown

mechanismDetails.selectivity: Highly selective for NNMT over other methyltransferases
primaryEffects: Increases intracellular NAD+ levels in adipose tissue
Enhances sirtuin activity through NAD+ elevation
mechanismDetails.tissueSpecificity: Effects most pronounced in white adipose tissue where NNMT expression is highest

knownConcerns: Potential cardiovascular effects (NNMT may protect endothelium)
Unknown effects on other NNMT-expressing tissues (liver, muscle, brain)
Long-term effects on NAD+ homeostasis unknown
safetyConsiderations.preclinicalSafety: Limited toxicology data; well-tolerated in short-term mouse studies
safetyConsiderations.regulatoryWarnings: Not approved for human use by any regulatory agency

notes: 5-Amino-1MQ is technically a small molecule, not a peptide, but is often marketed alongside peptides in research chemical markets. It targets NNMT, an enzyme that affects NAD+ metabolism in adipose tissue. The foundational research from Bhupendra Patel's laboratory at University of Texas Medical Branch established NNMT as a therapeutic target, with the 2014 Nature paper by Kraus et al. being the landmark study. While animal data shows promise for obesity and metabolic disease, NO human clinical trials have been published. The compound remains a research tool only.
